name: AgenticPipelineIngestor
observations: Enterprise-grade document processing pipeline with AI-driven decision making for content routing
Python 3.11+, FastAPI 0.104+, Pydantic 2.5+, PostgreSQL 15+, Redis 7+, SQLAlchemy 2.0+
Follows Spec-Driven Development (SDD) and API-First architecture
Status: Phase 4 Complete (Enterprise Features) as of 2026-02-16
83 Python source files, 20 test files, 1309-line spec document
Target: 20GB/day throughput, 10% near-realtime + 90% batch processing
Uses litellm for LLM abstraction (Azure OpenAI primary, OpenRouter fallback)
Build system: hatchling, linting: ruff (line-length=100, double quotes), type checking: mypy strict mode
Testing: pytest with asyncio_mode=auto, schemathesis for contract tests, hypothesis for property-based
Docker + docker-compose for local dev, AKS for Azure production deployment
7-stage pipeline: Ingest -> Detect -> Parse -> Enrich -> Quality -> Transform -> Output
PipelineExecutor (src/core/pipeline.py) manages stage execution with PipelineContext for data sharing
PipelineStage ABC with name property, execute() method, on_success()/on_failure() hooks
IngestStage: file validation, staging to /tmp/pipeline/staging/{job_id}, SHA-256 hash
DetectStage: uses ContentDetector for PDF analysis (text ratio, image ratio, font info)
ParseStage: primary/fallback parser with AgenticDecisionEngine for intelligent selection
EnrichStage: LLM-powered entity extraction and document classification
QualityStage: QualityAssessor with configurable thresholds, auto-retry recommendations
TransformStage: fixed-size and semantic chunking strategies, embedding generation placeholder
OutputStage: routes to configured destinations via PluginRegistry
Pipeline uses OpenTelemetry spans per stage and Prometheus metrics for timing
name: 7StagePipeline

observations: Located at src/core/engine.py - main job lifecycle manager
OrchestrationEngine class with plugin_registry, llm_provider, dlq, router dependencies
In-memory job store (_active_jobs dict[UUID, Job]) - no DB persistence yet
Key methods: create_job(), process_job(), retry_job(), cancel_job(), move_job_to_dlq()
Global singleton pattern via get_engine()/set_engine() functions
Retry logic integrates with RetryStrategyType and RetryContext from src/core/retry.py
process_job() creates PipelineExecutor and delegates to executor.execute()
name: OrchestrationEngine

observations: Located at src/core/decisions.py - LLM-powered decision engine
AgenticDecisionEngine: base class with select_parser(), decide_retry(), analyze_error()
AdvancedDecisionEngine: extends with ML-based decisions, historical data, adaptive thresholds
Parser selection uses LLM with JSON response format, falls back to rule-based decisions
Rule-based fallback: SCANNED_PDF->azure_ocr, IMAGE->azure_ocr, MIXED_PDF->docling+fallback, TEXT->docling
Retry decision based on quality score: <0.3->fallback_parser, 0.3-0.5->depends, >0.5->no retry
Key dataclasses: ParserSelection, RetryDecision, DecisionContext, DecisionResult
optimize_quality_thresholds() suggests thresholds based on historical QualityScore data
name: AgenticDecisionEngine
PluginSystem
observations: Base classes in src/plugins/base.py: BasePlugin, SourcePlugin, ParserPlugin, DestinationPlugin
Key data classes: PluginMetadata, Connection, SourceFile, RetrievedFile, ParsingResult, TransformedData, WriteResult
Protocols: StreamingSource, AsyncParser, BatchDestination for optional capabilities
PluginRegistry (src/plugins/registry.py): central registry with register/get/list methods
PluginLoader (src/plugins/loaders.py): auto-discovery from entry points and modules
Source plugins: S3Source, AzureBlobSource, SharePointSource
Parser plugins: DoclingParser, AzureOCRParser, CSVParser, JSONParser, XMLParser, EmailParser
Destination plugins: CogneeDestination, GraphRAGDestination, Neo4jDestination, PineconeDestination, WeaviateDestination
SourcePlugin has authorize() method for source-level authorization (S3 IAM, SharePoint permissions)

name: LLMProvider
observations: Located at src/llm/provider.py - litellm-based multi-provider LLM abstraction
LLMProvider class: initializes litellm Router with model list from config
Fallback chain: Azure GPT-4 -> OpenRouter Claude-3 -> Azure GPT-3.5
Methods: chat_completion(), simple_completion(), json_completion(), health_check()
ChatMessage dataclass with system/user/assistant factory methods
ChatCompletionResponse dataclass with from_litellm() class method
Full OpenTelemetry tracing with gen_ai.* span attributes per OpenTelemetry semantic conventions
Prometheus metrics recording for LLM requests (model, operation, status, latency, tokens)
Configuration via config/llm.yaml with env var resolution, loaded by LLMConfig in src/llm/config.py
Model groups: 'agentic-decisions' (GPT-4), 'enrichment' (GPT-3.5), 'classification'

name: AuthSystem
observations: Located at src/auth/ - comprehensive auth framework added in Phase 4
Multiple auth methods: API Key (api_key.py), JWT (jwt.py), OAuth2/OIDC (oauth2.py), Azure AD (azure_ad.py)
RBAC system (rbac.py): admin (all), operator (READ/CREATE/CANCEL/RETRY), developer (READ/CREATE_JOBS), viewer (READ)
FastAPI dependencies in src/auth/dependencies.py for route protection
Auth routes at /api/v1/auth/: login, token/refresh, me, logout, oauth2/authorize, oauth2/callback, api-keys CRUD
JWT token creation, validation, refresh via JWTHandler
API key generation with SHA-256 hashing via APIKeyAuth

name: AuditAndLineage
observations: Audit logging: src/audit/ with AuditEvent models and AuditLogger
Event types: JOB_CREATED/STARTED/COMPLETED/FAILED, AUTH_LOGIN/FAILED, API_KEY_CREATED/REVOKED, etc.
InMemoryAuditStore for dev, with Protocol-based storage backend pattern
Export capabilities: JSON, CSV, NDJSON formats
Data lineage: src/lineage/ with DataLineageTracker and InMemoryLineageStore
Tracks transformations through all 7 pipeline stages with input/output SHA-256 hashes
LineageGraph representation for visualization
Data retention: src/retention/manager.py with configurable policies (raw 30d, processed 90d, metadata 7yr, audit 7yr)
Audit routes at /api/v1/audit/: logs, export, summary, events/types
Lineage routes at /api/v1/lineage/{job_id}: graph, summary, verify, stages

name: Observability
observations: OpenTelemetry distributed tracing: src/observability/tracing.py with get_tracer(), start_pipeline_stage_span()
GenAI-specific spans: src/observability/genai_spans.py following OpenTelemetry semantic conventions
Prometheus metrics: src/observability/metrics.py with MetricsManager class
Metrics: ingestion_jobs_created_total, ingestion_jobs_completed_total, ingestion_job_duration_seconds, etc.
Structured logging: src/observability/logging.py using structlog
Middleware: src/observability/middleware.py for request/response instrumentation
Configuration: OTEL_ env prefix, config/otel-collector-config.yaml, config/prometheus.yml
Located at src/config.py - Pydantic Settings-based configuration
Main Settings class with nested sub-settings: database, redis, opensearch, azure, security, processing, observability, llm_yaml
Env var support with nested delimiter (__), .env file loading
DatabaseSettings: PostgreSQL async with pool config
SecuritySettings: JWT secret, algorithm, API key header, rate limiting, CORS
ProcessingSettings: max file size (100MB), max pages (1000), allowed MIME types, retry config
ObservabilitySettings: OTEL endpoint, Prometheus port, log level/format
Global settings instance via @lru_cache get_settings()
name: ConfigSettings

observations: Located at src/db/models.py - SQLAlchemy 2.0 models with type annotations
Tables: Job, JobDestination, PipelineConfig, SourceConfig, DestinationConfig, DataLineage, AuditLog
Phase 4 additions: User table (role, auth_provider, is_active, profile fields) and APIKey table (key_hash, permissions, rate_limit)
Async PostgreSQL support via asyncpg
Alembic-ready for migrations (but migrations not yet created)
name: DBModels
APILayer
observations: OpenAPI 3.1 spec at api/openapi.yaml (850+ lines, 28 endpoints)
FastAPI app entry at src/main.py
Pydantic models at src/api/models.py (50+ models including Job, JobCreateRequest, PipelineConfig, ContentDetectionResult, etc.)
Route handlers: src/api/routes/ with health.py, auth.py, audit.py, lineage.py, dlq.py, bulk.py
Dependencies at src/api/dependencies.py for DI
Key endpoints: POST /api/v1/jobs, GET /api/v1/jobs/{id}, POST /api/v1/upload, GET /health
SSE events at /api/v1/jobs/{id}/events for real-time job tracking
SDK generation via sdks/generate.py for Python and TypeScript

name: CoreAdditionalModules
observations: src/core/retry.py: RetryContext, RetryStrategyType enum, RetryStrategy implementations, get_retry_registry()
src/core/dlq.py: DeadLetterQueue for failed jobs with enqueue/dequeue/reprocess, get_dlq()
src/core/routing.py: DestinationRouter for multi-destination output routing, get_router()
src/core/healing.py: Self-healing mechanisms with exponential backoff and jitter
src/core/learning.py: Learning feedback loop for processing optimization
src/core/optimizations.py: Performance optimizations
src/core/detection.py: ContentDetector with PDFAnalyzer and ImageAnalyzer
src/core/quality.py: QualityAssessor, TextQualityAnalyzer, StructureQualityAnalyzer, QualityScore
src/core/entity_extraction.py: Entity extraction module
src/core/webhooks.py: Webhook handling with HMAC signatures
src/core/enrichment/advanced.py: Advanced enrichment module
src/core/graphrag/: Knowledge graph and community detection

name: TestSuite
observations: 20 test files across unit, integration, contract, and e2e categories
Unit tests: test_config.py, test_plugins.py, test_llm.py in tests/unit/
Integration tests: test_api.py in tests/integration/
Contract tests: test_openapi.py using Schemathesis in tests/contract/
E2E tests: test_full_pipeline.py, test_auth_flow.py, test_retry_mechanisms.py, test_dlq_workflow.py, test_performance.py
Pipeline integration test: tests/test_pipeline_integration.py
Test markers: unit, integration, contract, e2e, performance, slow, auth, retry, dlq
Coverage: minimum 1% configured (target 80%), addopts excludes e2e and performance by default
pytest-asyncio for async test support, factory-boy and faker for test data
src/worker/main.py: WorkerService for background job processing
src/worker/processor.py: JobProcessor for processing jobs from queue with retry logic
Poll-based job processing with concurrent execution (configurable)
Plugin lifecycle management and graceful shutdown via signal handling
Support for single-job execution mode
name: WorkerSystem

observations: docker/Dockerfile: Multi-stage build, Python 3.11 slim, non-root user, health check
docker/docker-compose.yml: Services: api, worker, PostgreSQL, Redis, OpenSearch, optional litellm proxy
azure/aks-deployment.yaml: K8s deployment for AKS with 3 API replicas, 5-10 workers

config/otel-collector-config.yaml: OpenTelemetry Collector configuration
Makefile: Build targets for common operations

Phase 2 DONE: Core Pipeline - 7 pipeline stages, content detection, Docling+Azure OCR parsers, Cognee destination, agentic decisions, quality assessment, worker
Phase 3 DONE (implied): Agentic Features - decision engine, retry strategies, DLQ, multi-destination routing, S3/Azure Blob sources
Phase 4 DONE: Enterprise - OAuth2, RBAC, audit logging, data lineage, SharePoint, data retention
Phase 5 PENDING: Observability & Scale - OpenTelemetry enhancement, Azure deploy, performance optimization, 20GB/day
Phase 6 PENDING: Advanced Features - additional parsers, more destinations, entity extraction, bulk operations
Known gaps: DB migrations not created, in-memory storage for audit/lineage (needs PostgreSQL/OpenSearch backends), no token blacklist

name: AdvancedRAG_Techniques_2025
observations: Comprehensive research conducted on 25+ advanced RAG techniques from 2024-2025
Research date: February 28, 2026
Project has highly sophisticated existing RAG implementation with 7-stage pipeline
Identified 15 cutting-edge approaches NOT yet implemented

name: Existing_RAG_Implementation
observations: AgenticRAG router with self-correction in src/rag/router.py
Query classification with 5 types (factual, analytical, comparative, vague, multi-hop)
4 chunking strategies: semantic, hierarchical, fixed, agentic
Contextual retrieval with 3 strategies: parent document, window, hierarchical
Hybrid search with RRF and weighted fusion
Cross-encoder re-ranking with 4 models (MiniLM, Electra, TinyBERT, BGE)
HyDE (Hypothetical Document Embeddings) implementation
GraphRAG with knowledge graph building and community detection
Multi-layer caching: L1 Redis, L2 Postgres, L3 Semantic
Comprehensive evaluation framework with MRR, NDCG, BERTScore, faithfulness
Vector stores: PostgreSQL/pgvector, Pinecone, Weaviate, Neo4j, Cognee
Multi-modal embeddings: dense + sparse vectors (BM25-compatible)
Query rewriting with @knowledgebase trigger
Self-correction logic with quality thresholds

name: Late_Interaction_Models_ColBERT
observations: ColBERT maintains token-level representations instead of single-vector embeddings
Uses late interaction scoring with MaxSim operations
Jina-ColBERT-v2 released 2024 with 50% storage reduction
Col-Bandit algorithm for query-time pruning (2026) - reduces FLOPs by 5x
Significant accuracy improvement: +15-25% over traditional dense retrieval
Trade-off: higher storage but better accuracy
Key innovation: decomposes relevance into token-level computations
Compatible with multi-modal retrieval (ColPali for documents)
Paper: arXiv:2408.16672 (Jina-ColBERT-v2), arXiv:2602.02827 (Col-Bandit)
NOT IMPLEMENTED in current project

name: LightRAG_NanoGraphRAG
observations: LightRAG: 10x faster alternative to Microsoft GraphRAG
Released late 2024, actively maintained
Simple, fast retrieval-augmented generation with graph structure
Supports multiple storage backends: NetworkX, Neo4j, Oracle
Query modes: naive, local, global, hybrid
Incremental document insertion support
FastAPI server implementation available
Graph visualization capabilities
Multi-file type support: PDF, DOC, PPT, CSV via textract
GitHub: github.com/HKUDS/LightRAG
NOT IMPLEMENTED - current GraphRAG uses external API approach
10x faster than Microsoft GraphRAG
Local embedded library (not API service)
Storage backends: NetworkX, Neo4j, Oracle, PostgreSQL
FastAPI server available for REST API
Multi-file support via textract
GitHub: HKUDS/LightRAG
Best for: Self-hosted, speed-critical, privacy-focused
Truly local/embedded - no external dependencies
Easy pip install: pip install lightrag-hku
Simple Python API
Active development with strong community

name: HippoRAG
observations: Neurobiologically-inspired long-term memory for LLMs
Mimics hippocampal indexing theory from human memory
Combines LLM + Knowledge Graph + Personalized PageRank
Single-step retrieval achieves multi-hop reasoning capability
Outperforms SOTA by up to 20% on multi-hop QA
10-30x cheaper and 6-13x faster than iterative retrieval (IRCoT)
HippoRAG 2 (ICML 2025) adds continual learning capabilities
Pattern separation and pattern completion from memory theory
Uses OpenIE for triple extraction during indexing
Node specificity instead of IDF for local signals
Paper: arXiv:2405.14831 (NeurIPS 2024)
GitHub: github.com/OSU-NLP-Group/HippoRAG
NOT IMPLEMENTED - completely different paradigm from existing RAG
Single-step multi-hop retrieval (vs iterative)
10-30x cheaper than IRCoT iterative retrieval
6-13x faster than multi-step approaches
HippoRAG 2 adds continual learning (ICML 2025)
Pattern separation + pattern completion memory model
Uses OpenIE for triple extraction
GitHub: OSU-NLP-Group/HippoRAG
Best for: Research, complex reasoning, explainability
Research codebase - less production polish
Novel paradigm requiring different architecture

name: SelfRAG_CorrectiveRAG
observations: Self-RAG uses reflection tokens for self-critique (arXiv:2310.11511)
Adaptive retrieval: decides when to retrieve, what to retrieve
Generation tokens control output quality
Critique tokens evaluate factual accuracy
Corrective RAG (CRAG) evaluates retrieval quality and triggers web search fallback
LEAF: Learning and Evaluation Augmented by Fact-checking (arXiv:2410.23526)
Working memory approach for fact tracking (ACL 2025)
Self-RAG 7B/13B outperforms ChatGPT on factuality
NOT FULLY IMPLEMENTED - project has quality assessment but not reflection tokens

name: RAG_Fusion_MultiQuery
observations: Generates multiple query variations from single user query
Retrieves documents for each variation
Reranks and fuses results using RRF or similar
Better coverage for ambiguous queries
Captures different query intents
Similar to existing query rewriting but with fusion
Could extend current hybrid search implementation
NOT IMPLEMENTED as dedicated fusion strategy

name: AdaptiveRAG
observations: Dynamic strategy selection based on query complexity
Routes simple queries to fast paths, complex to thorough paths
Query complexity classification: simple vs complex
Efficiency gains by avoiding heavy processing for easy queries
Complements existing AgenticRAG router
Could integrate with existing query classification
NOT IMPLEMENTED as explicit adaptive routing

name: Speculative_RAG
observations: Draft-then-verify approach
Generate draft answer first with limited context
Verify and enhance with targeted retrieval
Efficiency + quality combined
Good for fact-heavy domains
Research area - implementation complexity high
NOT IMPLEMENTED

name: FLARE_RAG
observations: Forward-Looking Active REtrieval
Active retrieval during generation process
Predicts what information will be needed next
Best for long-form content generation
Retrieves proactively before generation stalls
Paper: arXiv:2305.06983
NOT IMPLEMENTED

name: MultiModal_RAG_Vision
observations: Enhanced Multimodal RAG-LLM for document images (arXiv:2412.20927)
SURf: Selective Utilization of Retrieved visual info (arXiv:2409.14083)
Vision-language model integration
Unified embedding spaces for text and images
Cross-modal retrieval capabilities
ColPali/ColQwen2 for document image retrieval with late interaction
Plancraft dataset for evaluation
Project has basic multi-modal but not advanced vision-language RAG
NOT FULLY IMPLEMENTED - could enhance existing GraphRAG with visual nodes

name: Structured_RAG_SQL_KG
observations: Combines structured data (SQL, KG) with unstructured text
ER-RAG: Entity-Relationship unified modeling (arXiv:2504.06271)
RAGONITE: SQL + verbalized KG facts (arXiv:2412.17558)
KRAIL framework for legal/regulatory applications
Multi-source heterogeneous data integration
Could enhance existing hybrid search with structured queries
NOT IMPLEMENTED as explicit structured data integration

name: Table_RAG
observations: Specialized for tabular data retrieval
TableRAG: Million-token table understanding
Cell-level retrieval for large tables
Important for financial, scientific, business data
NOT IMPLEMENTED - project handles documents but not specialized table RAG

name: Temporal_RAG
observations: ChronoQA: Temporal reasoning in RAG (arXiv:2508.12282)
Time-aware retrieval and reasoning
Handles time-based queries and evolving knowledge
NOT IMPLEMENTED - no temporal awareness in current RAG

name: Privacy_Preserving_RAG
observations: RAG with differential privacy guarantees (arXiv:2412.19291)
Privacy-preserving retrieval systems
Important for enterprise applications
NOT IMPLEMENTED

name: SPLADE_Sparse_Retrieval
observations: Learned sparse retrieval using BERT
Combines benefits of BM25 and neural approaches
Mistral-SPLADE: LLM-based sparse retrieval (2024)
Compatible with existing PostgreSQL inverted index
Could enhance current sparse embedding approach

name: GraphRAG_Benchmark_2025
observations: GraphRAG-Bench accepted at ICLR 2026
Comprehensive benchmark for GraphRAG evaluation
Multiple datasets: MultiHop-RAG, HotpotQA, 2Wiki, MuSiQue
LinearRAG: Relation-free graph construction (ICLR 2026)
LogicRAG: Logical reasoning-based retrieval (AAAI 2026)

name: Implementation_Priority_Matrix
observations: TIER 1 (High Impact): ColBERT/Late Interaction, Corrective RAG (CRAG), Adaptive RAG, Long-Context RAG
TIER 2 (Specialized): LightRAG (faster GraphRAG), Table RAG, FLARE, RAG with Feedback Loops
TIER 3 (Advanced): HippoRAG, Speculative RAG, Multi-modal Vision RAG, Temporal RAG
Current project maturity: Enterprise-grade with room for cutting-edge additions
Easiest to implement: Adaptive RAG (extends existing), CRAG (quality framework)
Highest impact: ColBERT (accuracy), LightRAG (speed), HippoRAG (multi-hop capability)

name: Cognee
observations: API-based memory and knowledge graph system for AI agents by topoteretes
Combines knowledge graph structure with vector embeddings
Supports multi-modal data (text, images, structured data)
Provides REST API for all operations
Production-ready with enterprise features
Primarily cloud/SaaS service but can be self-hosted
Used as primary destination in AgenticPipelineIngestor project
Best for enterprise production deployments with managed service preference
Open-source knowledge engine for AI agent memory
Combines vector search + graph databases + self-improvement
API-based service (cloud or self-hosted)
Multi-modal support: text, images, audio, structured data
Pythonic data pipelines for 30+ data sources
Modular pipelines and built-in search endpoints
Production-ready with enterprise support available
GitHub: topoteretes/cognee
Paper: arXiv:2505.24478
Best for: Managed service, multi-modal, enterprise use cases
Not truly local/embedded - still API-based architecture
Current project has API-based Cognee destination plugin
CORRECTED: Cognee is primarily a LOCAL/EMBEDDED Python library
Installation: pip install cognee (local library, not just API)
Works 100% locally with data stored on device
Default local storage: SQLite (metadata), LanceDB (vectors), Kuzu (graph)
Can use local LLMs via Ollama (no API required)
Hosted API service is just a deployment option, not the core product
Architecture: Python library with modular backends
Graph backends: Kuzu (default), Neo4j, FalkorDB, Memgraph, NetworkX
Vector backends: LanceDB (default), PGVector, Qdrant, Redis, ChromaDB
Relational: SQLite (default), PostgreSQL
Core operations: add(), cognify(), search() - all local
Also offers hosted cloud version but core is open-source library
Paper: arXiv:2505.24478 on optimizing KG-LLM interface

name: LightRAG
observations: Open-source Python library for fast GraphRAG
10x faster than Microsoft's GraphRAG
Dual-level retrieval: low-level (entity) + high-level (community)
Supports incremental updates without full rebuild
Multiple storage backends: Neo4j, NetworkX, PostgreSQL
Four query modes: naive, local, global, hybrid
Embedded library - runs locally on your infrastructure
Best for speed-critical applications and data privacy requirements

name: GraphRAG_Comparison_2025
observations: Comprehensive comparison of Cognee, LightRAG, and HippoRAG completed February 2026
Cognee ranked best for enterprise production (9/10)
LightRAG ranked best for self-hosted speed-critical use (8/10)
HippoRAG ranked best for research and complex reasoning (6/10)
Cognee is API-based, LightRAG and HippoRAG are embedded libraries
Research report saved to shared/research-reports/cognee-vs-lightrag-vs-hipporag-comparison.md
CORRECTED COMPARISON: All three (LightRAG, HippoRAG, Cognee) offer local/embedded options
Cognee: pip install cognee, local SQLite/LanceDB/Kuzu by default
LightRAG: pip install lightrag-hku, local NetworkX/Neo4j/PostgreSQL
HippoRAG: pip install hipporag, local graph with PPR
All three support local LLMs via Ollama
All three are open-source Python libraries
Key differentiator is architecture, not local vs API

name: Cognee_pgvector_schema
observations: Created migration 015_add_cognee_pgvector_schema.py in migrations/versions/
Creates three tables: cognee_vectors, cognee_documents, cognee_entities
Uses pgvector extension with VECTOR(1536) for OpenAI text-embedding-3-small
IVFFlat index with lists=100 for approximate nearest neighbor search
Schema documentation created at shared/db-schema.md
Migration merges heads 012, 013, 014 into 015

name: cognee_vectors
observations: Stores chunk embeddings for semantic search
Columns: id (UUID PK), chunk_id, document_id, dataset_id, embedding (VECTOR(1536)), metadata (JSONB), created_at, updated_at
Indexes: idx_cognee_vectors_chunk_id, idx_cognee_vectors_document_id, idx_cognee_vectors_dataset_id (B-tree), idx_cognee_vectors_embedding (IVFFlat cosine)

name: cognee_documents
observations: Document metadata storage for hybrid queries
Columns: id (UUID PK), document_id (unique), dataset_id, title, source_path, content_hash, metadata (JSONB), chunk_count, entity_count, created_at, updated_at
Indexes: idx_cognee_documents_dataset_id, idx_cognee_documents_document_id (B-tree)

name: cognee_entities
observations: Entity mirror from Neo4j for hybrid graph + vector queries
Columns: id (UUID PK), entity_id (unique), name, type, description, document_id, dataset_id, metadata (JSONB), created_at
Indexes: idx_cognee_entities_name, idx_cognee_entities_type, idx_cognee_entities_document_id (B-tree)
